_comment: Tunables for how the companion FEELS. Distances are in pixels, speeds in px/sec, intervals in seconds. 'personality' is the legacy 0..1 fallback; 'traits' are the live, drifting versions seeded from it. 'drift' controls how slowly the companion comes to reflect how you play. Tune all of this to change the vibe without touching code.
_wander_comment: Self-directed wandering: when settled near the player, it may stroll to a nearby prop on its own. chance is per second, scaled by the curiosity trait AND damped by bond (a more bonded companion strikes out less). cooldown keeps it occasional.
_bond_comment: The relationship itself (0..1), persistent across sessions. It starts at 'init' (a fresh companion is its own creature) and grows: grow_per_sec always while present, grow_per_sec_near extra while you stay within follow_near, grow_per_interaction on each shared examine. With these values an ever-near player reaches a strong bond in roughly 10-12 minutes, and it is saved so a returning companion already knows you.
_balance_comment: How bond tips the wander-vs-follow contest. Self-wander scores wander_score_low at bond 0 (its own agenda) fading to wander_score_high at bond 1. Following scores follow_eager_low..follow_eager_high by bond, PLUS a distance leash (0 at follow_near ramping to follow_leash at follow_far) so it never lets you get truly far. A player-triggered look is fixed well above all of these.

traits._comment: init must match personality above so first-run feel is unchanged. min/max bound how far a trait can drift; drift_rate is how fast (per second toward target) — keep it small so change is felt, not seen.

drift._comment: warmup_seconds: watch this long before drifting at all. reference_* set what counts as 'a lot' of roaming / examining. targets: weighted blend of play signals (explore, together, engage) each trait drifts toward.
